name: Choc Hazelnut Mascarpone Torte 2
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the caramelized hazelnuts and hazelnut praline:
a. Lightly spray a baking sheet with nonstick cooking spray.
b. In a small saucepan, combine sugar and water. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until sugar dissolves.
c. Increase heat to medium-high and continue cooking without stirring for 4-6 minutes until syrup caramelizes.
d. Remove pan from heat, add hazelnuts, toss to coat. Place hazelnuts at one end of baking sheet.
e. Stir remaining hot nuts into caramel, pour onto other end of baking sheet.
f. Cool praline for 30 minutes until hard. Reserve individual caramelized hazelnuts for decorating the cake.
g. Coarsely chop praline and process in food processor until finely ground. Transfer to bowl and set aside.
2. Make the framboise syrup:
a. In a small saucepan, combine water and sugar. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly with wooden spoon, until sugar dissolves.
b. Stop stirring; raise heat to medium-high and bring syrup to boil. Remove from heat and stir in framboise liqueur and vanilla.
3. Make the mascarpone cream filling:
a. In 4 1/2-quart bowl, beat together mascarpone cheese, sugar, and framboise liqueur at medium speed until combined (about 1 minute).
b. Gradually add heavy cream; increase speed to medium-high and beat until soft peaks form and mixture is spreadable.
c. Cover and refrigerate cream until ready to use.
4. Assemble the cake:
a. Cut cake lengthwise into 3 equal strips using long serrated knife.
b. Place one strip on wire rack, brush top with framboise syrup.
c. Spread mascarpone cream over moistened strip; cover with raspberries (2 1/3 cups) placed stem-side-down in even rows.
d. Scrape remaining mascarpone cream over raspberries and sprinkle ground praline over cream layer.
e. Place second cake strip on top, repeat layering process, and place final strip on wire rack.
5. Make the glaze:
a. Place chocolate in medium bowl.
b. In small saucepan, bring cream and corn syrup to gentle boil; pour hot mixture over chocolate.
c. Let stand 30 seconds, then gently whisk until smooth. Allow glaze to cool for 5 minutes.
d. Pour glaze over cake, covering completely. Garnish sides with ground praline while glaze is still wet.
6. Refrigerate cake for 5-10 minutes until glaze sets. Garnish top with caramelized hazelnuts.
